steps.title: Spot the consequence signal
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that Italian was historically the default for opera. The second sentence reveals that modern singers (marked by “today”) still encounter Italian terms. This is a direct consequence of the historical fact.

steps.title: Apply the consequence signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is cause-effect. Because Italian was the default language for opera scores for centuries, today's singers still encounter Italian terms when reading those scores. The blank needs a transition signaling that consequence.
  • Choice A — "However,": signals contrast — the second sentence would push back against the first. But singers encountering Italian terms is a direct result of the historical dominance of Italian, not a contradiction of it. ✗
  • Choice B — "Thus,": signals that the second sentence is a logical result of the first, fitting the cause-effect relationship perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — "Lastly,": signals the final item in an enumerated list. No prior list is being wrapped up. ✗
  • Choice D — "Granted,": signals a concession — acknowledging a point before countering it. No concession or counterargument is present. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
